eyebrow_text: Built on the official Saudi customs tariff
subtitle: Wrong or missing codes mean customs delays and hours of manual processing. Describe your product in plain words and get the correct 12-digit code in seconds, with duty rates, Arabic descriptions, and a clear rationale for every pick.
placeholder: What are you shipping? e.g. cold-pressed olive oil in 500 ml glass bottles…
mode_generate_desc: Describe your product, get the full 12-digit code
mode_expand_desc: Have a partial code? We complete it to 12 digits
mode_batch_desc: Upload an invoice, classify every line at once
drop_hint: …or click to browse · CSV or Excel accepted
manual_pick_title: Couldn't auto-select a code right now
manual_pick_body: Our ranking system is temporarily unavailable, so we couldn't pick one automatically. Here are the closest matches we found. Pick the best fit, or try again in a moment.
result.procedures.title: Required Procedures
err_generic: Something went wrong. Please try again.
err_empty: Describe a product to classify it.
ai_disclaimer: AI-generated suggestion. The submitted description is a legal declaration — verify it accurately describes your product before pasting into ZATCA.
step_reason: Picking the best match and verifying it
trace_not_found: Trace not found — the request id may have expired or been mistyped.
trace_score_tooltip: Search relevance, normalized to top match = 1.0
reason_long_single_valid_descendant: Only one leaf available under this prefix
reason_long_already_most_specific: Declared code is already most specific
reason_long_low_top_score: Search results were too weak to reason about
reason_long_small_top2_gap: Top candidates were too close to call
reason_long_ambiguous_top_candidates: Top candidates were too close to call
reason_long_guard_tripped: AI tried to invent a code — caught and discarded
reason_long_brand_not_recognised: Couldn't recognize the brand or model
reason_long_best_effort_heading: Returned heading-level code as best effort
stage_retrieval: Searched the ZATCA tariff codes library
stage_retrieval_summary: {count} candidates · top score {top} · gap to second {gap}
stage_evidence_pass: Evidence strong enough — proceeded to picker
stage_picker_guarded: Hallucination guard caught the picker's response — discarded
stage_best_effort_returned: Picker abstained → best-effort fallback returned a heading-level code
fb_err_reason_too_short: Reason must be at least 10 characters.
fb_err_corrected_required: Choose a candidate or enter a 12-digit code.
t2_glossary_rrf: RRF (Cormack et al., SIGIR 2009): a parameter-free method that ranks items by where they appear across multiple lists, ignoring raw scores. Now the default fuser in Elastic, Vespa, Weaviate, Qdrant, Pinecone.
t2_glossary_gate: A deterministic check that flags weak retrieval results before the picker runs. Doesn't always block — sometimes it just annotates the candidates with a warning.
t2_glossary_picker: The reasoning LLM that chooses ONE 12-digit code from the retrieved candidates and writes a rationale grounded in the General Interpretive Rules of HS classification.
t2_glossary_best_effort: Backup mode that returns a heading-level (HS-4 or HS-6) answer when the picker can't confidently land on a 12-digit leaf.
t2_cleanup_what: Strips brand names, SKUs, marketing copy, and other noise that retrieval can't act on. Keeps the product type, material, and intended use.
t2_retrieval_what: Three search methods run against the ZATCA catalog in parallel, each producing its own ranked list. The lists are fused via Reciprocal Rank Fusion into a single ranked candidate list. This list becomes the input to every downstream stage — if a code isn't here, the picker can't choose it.
t2_retrieval_methods_label: How we searched · 3 methods in parallel
t2_retrieval_method_vectors_desc: e5-small embeddings + cosine similarity. Catches semantic matches even when the words don't overlap (Arabic, synonyms, paraphrases).
t2_retrieval_method_bm25_desc: Postgres full-text. Ranks by exact word overlap with TF-IDF weighting. Catches brand names, SKUs, technical terms.
t2_retrieval_method_trigram_desc: Postgres pg_trgm. Three-character n-gram overlap. Catches typos and partial matches the other two methods miss.
t2_retrieval_signal_label: Ranking signal handed to the gate
t2_retrieval_next: The candidate list goes to the evidence gate, which decides whether the ranking is strong enough to send to the picker LLM.
t2_research_what: When the cleaned input is brand jargon or merchant shorthand, a reasoning LLM rewrites it into a canonical product phrase that retrieval can match. Skipped on inputs that are already clear.
t2_gate_what: Before retrieval results reach the picker, three signals must pass. If any fails, the picker is either skipped or warned.
t2_gate_decision_pass_body: All three signals pass. The picker LLM gets the candidate list to make the final choice.
t2_gate_decision_warn_body: The two top candidates are nearly tied. The picker still gets to decide, but with a flag asking it to disambiguate carefully — typically using GIR rules or unspecified-attribute defaults.
t2_gate_decision_fail_body: Retrieval didn't produce strong enough candidates to justify the picker's cost. The system will fall back to a heading-level answer.
t2_gate_next_pass: When retrieval is strong, the picker stage gets the candidates and the rationale instruction.
t2_gate_next_warn: When retrieval is tied (close gap) but candidates are otherwise plausible, the system still attempts the picker — the gate's job is to flag the tie, not block.
t2_gate_next_fail: The picker is skipped. Best-effort fallback runs to see if a confident heading-level (HS-4) answer is available.
t2_picker_what: A reasoning LLM call. Given the user input, the cleaned query, and the top candidates, the picker chooses exactly one 12-digit leaf and writes a rationale grounded in the GIR rulebook. A hallucination guard rejects any code not in the input candidate set.
t2_picker_check_in_set: Chosen code is in the candidate set
t2_picker_check_schema: Output JSON matches the picker schema
t2_picker_decision_guarded_body: The picker returned a code outside the candidate set. The guard rejected it. Best-effort fallback runs next.
t2_picker_decision_skipped_body: The evidence gate refused to send candidates to the picker.
t2_picker_next: Branch enumeration runs to confirm the chosen leaf has no narrower siblings worth promoting to.
t2_picker_next_skipped: The picker didn't run. Best-effort fallback gets the candidate list instead.
t2_best_effort_what: Triggered when the picker can't confidently choose a 12-digit leaf. Asks: "Is there a confident HEADING-level (HS-4 or HS-6) answer we could ship instead?" Returns a heading-padded code (e.g. 6402 → 640200000000) tagged for downstream review.
t2_best_effort_outcome_body: Promoted to accepted at heading level: {code}. Decision tagged: {reason}.
